tree.name: Nolan Traore
tree.note: French guard, drafted #19 with Milwaukee's 2025 1st acquired via NYK in the Mikal Bridges mega-trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2025 1st Round Pick (#19) via MIL
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: BKN traded Mikal Bridges, Keita Bates-Diop, Juan Pablo Vaulet, and a 2026 2nd to NYK for Bogdanovic, Diakite, Milton, NYK 2025 1st (#26), MIL 2025 1st (#19), BKN 2025 2nd (#36), NYK 2027 1st, NYK/MIL 2028 swap, NYK 2029 1st, and NYK 2031 1st. Brooklyn also received trade exceptions.
assetsGivenUp.note: Milwaukee's own 2025 1st-round pick. NYK acquired it and routed to BKN in the Bridges trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Mikal Bridges
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Acquired from Phoenix in Kevin Durant trade. BKN sent KD and TJ Warren to PHX for Cam Johnson, Mikal Bridges, 4 first-round picks, and pick swap rights.
assetsGivenUp.note: Villanova product, elite 3-and-D wing. Centerpiece BKN flipped to NYK for massive pick haul.

assetsGivenUp.name: Kevin Durant
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Sign-and-trade: Nets traded D'Angelo Russell, Treveon Graham, and Shabazz Napier to Warriors for Kevin Durant. Conditional 2020 1st did not convey; became 2025 2nd (Javon Small).

assetsGivenUp.name: D'Angelo Russell
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Nets traded Brook Lopez and the #27 pick (Kyle Kuzma) to Lakers for D'Angelo Russell and Timofey Mozgov.

assetsGivenUp.name: Brook Lopez
assetsGivenUp.note: Drafted #10 overall by New Jersey Nets in 2008. Franchise cornerstone for a decade.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2017 1st Round Pick (#27 via pick swap)
assetsGivenUp.note: BKN had the worst record in 2017 (#1 pick), but owed it to BOS from the KG/Pierce trade. BOS swapped #1 to PHI for #3 (Jayson Tatum). BKN got BOS's pick position (#27) via the swap. LAL drafted Kyle Kuzma.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2017 1st Round Pick (#1 overall)
assetsGivenUp.note: BKN's own pick — worst record in NBA, #1 overall. Owed to BOS from the 2013 KG/Pierce trade. BOS traded #1 to PHI for #3 (Jayson Tatum) and a future 1st. PHI drafted Markelle Fultz.

assetsGivenUp.name: Treveon Graham
assetsGivenUp.note: Signed as FA by BKN. Sent to GSW in KD sign-and-trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Shabazz Napier
assetsGivenUp.note: UConn guard, signed as free agent by BKN (2yr deal, July 2018). Sent to GSW in KD sign-and-trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: TJ Warren
assetsGivenUp.note: Signed as FA by BKN. Sent to PHX in Kevin Durant trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Keita Bates-Diop
assetsGivenUp.note: Signed as FA by BKN. Sent to NYK in Bridges trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Juan Pablo Vaulet
assetsGivenUp.note: Argentine forward, stash player. Draft rights acquired from Charlotte on draft night 2015. Later sent to IND (2021) then included in Bridges trade to NYK (2024).
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Charlotte traded Vaulet draft rights to BKN for cash ($880,000), a 2018 2nd (least favorable of BKN/CLE, became Arnoldas Kulboka), and a 2019 2nd (became Talen Horton-Tucker).

assetsGivenUp.name: 2018 2nd Round Pick (#55, least favorable of BKN/CLE)
assetsGivenUp.note: Sent to Charlotte for Vaulet draft rights. CHA used it to draft Arnoldas Kulboka.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2019 2nd Round Pick (#46)
assetsGivenUp.note: Sent to Charlotte for Vaulet draft rights. Became Talen Horton-Tucker.

assetsGivenUp.name: Cash considerations ($880,000)
assetsGivenUp.note: Cash sent to Charlotte for Vaulet draft rights.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2026 2nd Round Pick (least favorable)
assetsGivenUp.note: Sent to NYK in Bridges trade.
